theme: "In a world where the Fates have grown tired of their own prophecies, a mortal hero emerges to challenge the Oracle's latest decree: 'The Weaver shall unravel the very fabric of fate itself.' Set in ancient Delphi, amidst the crumbling temples and whispered secrets of the gods, our protagonist must navigate the treacherous landscape of divine politics, prophesied doom, and their own uncertain destiny."
visual_concept: "Visualize an atmospheric, moody landscape of ancient Delphi at twilight. The Oracle's temple looms in the background, its crumbling columns shrouded in a veil of mist. In the foreground, our mortal hero stands atop a worn stone pedestal, their eyes fixed intently on the Fates' sacred Loom. The loom itself glows with an ethereal light, as if the very fabric of fate is beginning to unravel. To one side, a figure shrouded in shadows whispers secrets to the Oracle's statue; to the other, a pair of winged horses, symbols of fate and prophecy, gallop towards the horizon, their manes and tails flowing like mist. The sky above is a deep, foreboding indigo, with stars beginning to twinkle like diamonds scattered across the velvet expanse."
character_context: "Meet Kaelin 'The Unraveler' of Delphi, a 25-year-old mortal with an uncanny ability to see the threads of fate that bind gods and mortals alike. Born into a family of humble priests, Kaelin has always felt an otherworldly connection to the Oracle's temple, where they would often sneak in at night to watch the Fates weave their tapestries. Haunted by prophetic dreams and plagued by an unshakeable sense of destiny, Kaelin is both drawn to and terrified by the Oracle's latest decree: that they shall unravel the very fabric of fate itself. With a troubled past and a penchant for defying the gods, Kaelin stands poised on the precipice of greatness - or catastrophe - as they prepare to face their own uncertain destiny."
caption_hook: The Oracle just auto-generated a bad email.
post_body: Because that's exactly what I wanted to do with my life - defy the gods.
share_cta: Send this to your friend who's always asking for fortune cookies
hook_score_reason: Slightly shorter and more concise, still conveys the idea of a prophecy gone wrong
scene_anchor: the Oracle's statue whispering to a figure in the shadows
selection_reason: Clear, concise hook that adds humor to a relatable situation and invites sharing with friends.

candidates.post_body: I mean, I knew I was destined for greatness... but this is just too much.

candidates.caption_hook: Zeus just sent me a strongly-worded tweet about how I'm not living up to my 'god-given potential
candidates.post_body: I mean, I was trying to live my best life... but it turns out that's just code for 'you're not doing enough

candidates.caption_hook: When the Fates say 'you're destined for greatness' and you realize it's just a metaphor for being slowly unrav
candidates.post_body: Just remember: even in the face of cosmic uncertainty, you can still choose to weave your own tapestry.

candidates.caption_hook: When you're trying to build a relationship with your destiny and it's just not reciprocating
candidates.post_body: I mean, I know the Fates are supposed to be all-knowing... but honestly, they have no idea what they're doing.

candidates.caption_hook: Why did I get a prophetic dream about Netflix? Because they're trying to binge-watch my life too
candidates.post_body: Just me, trying to navigate the depths of fate while also keeping up with my watchlist.
